summary: Task 02 cleanly consolidates the duplicated rate formatter. rateUnitParts is a correct unexported core; RateUnit stays a byte-identical thin wrapper (field+unit, '9999MB/s'); RateUnitPrefixed returns field+" "+prefix+unit with the space and prefix preserved. rateField is fully deleted with all four call sites repointed to identical args, no stale references, and no maxFit promotion-boundary drift. Build and tests pass.

notes.detail: Verified maxFit boundary by hand against the pre-refactor rateField body. Base case (v=9999, width 4): Ceil=9999<=maxFit(9999) -> ReserveWidth(9999,4)='9999', unit base -> '9999 rMB/s'. Promotion case (v=10000): Ceil=10000>9999 -> ReserveWidth(Ceil(10000/1024)=10,4)='  10', unit high -> '  10 wGB/s'. Both match the hardcoded golden literals in TestRateUnitPrefixed. No off-by-one: the '<=' comparison and divisor selection live unchanged in the shared core, so RateUnit and RateUnitPrefixed share the exact same threshold.

grep -rn rateField across top/ and internal/ returns only three hits, all inside the explanatory comment of TestRateUnitPrefixed; the function itself and its doc comment are gone. No stale call sites.

All four sites in renderSysstatVerbose use identical (v, family, prefix, width) arg order matching the original rateField calls: disk r (d.Rsectors,FamilyDisk,'r',4), disk w (d.Wsectors,FamilyDisk,'w',4), net r (n.Rbytes/1024/128,FamilyNet,'r',4), net w (n.Tbytes/1024/128,FamilyNet,'w',4). The top/stat_test.go verbose goldens ('1135 rMB/s', '1546 wMB/s', '4345 rMbps', '6543 wMbps') are unchanged and pass.
Return names field, unit and params v, family, prefix, width do not shadow Go builtins (field/unit are not builtins). revive redefines-builtin-id is satisfied; go build ./... clean.

RateUnitPrefixed and rateUnitParts both have accurate doc comments describing the separator/prefix layout and the shared boundary logic. RateUnit's original doc comment is retained verbatim above the wrapper, preserving the feature-010 API documentation. Idiomatic Go: named returns used appropriately, no new imports, no dead vars.
